page: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Skylake Core Processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware includes 16 physical cores with single-thread execution per core, 58.0 GB of system memory providing 3.62 GB per core, and a bundled 1024 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. It features 16 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across multiple domains. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests score 1884.79 and 14058.95 respectively, placing them in the average tier. While cached small-block read memory bandwidth is average at 907.89 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak to poor, measuring 24.85 GB/sec and 13.66 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly at 30.89 GB/sec, but static web serving throughput is weak at 6.08 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced resource density, this server offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial local NVMe storage and moderate memory without needing dedicated CPU cores. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, as reflected by its average Passmark database score of 5215.72 and Redis throughput of 11.03 million operations per second.
description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 16 shared x86_64 Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 58.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1024 GB NVMe SSD. The architecture provides 3.62 GB of memory per core and includes 16 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14058.95 and an average Redis SET throughput of 11.03 million operations per second. However, memory bandwidth for uncached large blocks is weak, and static web serving throughput is limited to 6.08 GB/sec. This server represents a resource-dense, cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from high-capacity local storage and balanced memory, such as database operations, general-purpose web applications, and multi-threaded file processing, without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 16 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs, 58.0 GB of RAM, and a 1024 GB NVMe SSD, suitable for general-purpose workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server with 16 shared Intel vCPUs, 58.0 GB RAM, and a 1024 GB NVMe SSD for database and general workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU server featuring sixteen cores, balanced memory, and high-capacity NVMe storage for general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
58.0 GB of system memory and 1024 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 30.89 GB/sec
Average Redis SET performance of 11.03 million operations per second
Best suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and general-purpose applications
